L1.area: HTTP cookie management and data persistence layer

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where user-controlled cookie values are deserialized using an unsafe mechanism without proper validation, allowing PHP object injection. An attacker can craft a malicious cookie payload that, when processed by the application, triggers arbitrary code execution through magic methods during the deserialization process.

L3.area: HTTP cookie management and data persistence layer
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where user-controlled cookie values are deserialized using an unsafe mechanism without proper validation, allowing PHP object injection. An attacker can craft a malicious cookie payload that, when processed by the application, triggers arbitrary code execution through magic methods during the deserialization process.

ground_truth.reason: The cookie retrieval method uses PHP's unserialize() on user-controlled cookie values without validation, enabling PHP object injection and arbitrary code execution via magic methods like __wakeup().
